questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Scientists collected fallen acorns that each housed a colony of the ant species P. ohioensis and analyzed each colony's structure. For any of these colonies, if the colony has $x$ worker ants, the equation $y = 0.67x + 2.6$, where $20 \leq x \leq 110$, gives the predicted number of larvae, $y$, in the colony. If one of these colonies has 53 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?
questions.questionText: Scientists collected fallen acorns that each housed a colony of the ant species P. ohioensis and analyzed each colony's structure. For any of these colonies, if the colony has $x$ worker ants, the equation $y = 0.67x + 2.6$, where $20 \leq x \leq 110$, gives the predicted number of larvae, $y$, in the colony. If one of these colonies has 53 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 40º and the measure of angle $E$ is 95º. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 40º and the measure of angle $S$ is 95º. Which of the following additional pieces of information is needed to determine whether triangle $DEF$ is similar to triangle $RST$?
questions.questionText: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 40º and the measure of angle $E$ is 95º. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 40º and the measure of angle $S$ is 95º. Which of the following additional pieces of information is needed to determine whether triangle $DEF$ is similar to triangle $RST$?

choices.text: The measure of angle $F$ and the measure of angle $T$

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x - 4)^2 + 5$ gives a toy car's height above the ground $f(x)$, in inches, $x$ seconds after it started moving on an elevated track, where $0 \leq x \leq 10$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x - 4)^2 + 5$ gives a toy car's height above the ground $f(x)$, in inches, $x$ seconds after it started moving on an elevated track, where $0 \leq x \leq 10$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: The toy car's minimum height was 5 inches above the ground.
The toy car's minimum height was 4 inches above the ground.
The toy car's height was 5 inches above the ground when it started moving.
The toy car's height was 4 inches above the ground when it started moving.

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function $f(t)=30,000(2)^{\frac{t}{370}}$ gives the number of bacteria in a population $t$ minutes after an initial observation. How much time, in minutes, does it take for the number of bacteria in the population to double?
questions.questionText: The function $f(t)=30,000(2)^{\frac{t}{370}}$ gives the number of bacteria in a population $t$ minutes after an initial observation. How much time, in minutes, does it take for the number of bacteria in the population to double?

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
A bin contains a mixture of T-shirts for two sports teams. The table shows the number of T-shirts in the bin, classified by size and sports team.
One T-shirt from the bin will be selected at random. What is the probability of selecting a T-shirt that is medium, given that the T-shirt is a Flames T-shirt?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A polygonal chain is a connected series of line segments whose length is defined as the sum of the lengths of the line segments it is made of. A polygonal chain with a length of 33 centimeters (cm) is extended by adding line segments that each have a length of $8 - r$ cm. Which function $f$ represents the total length, in cm, of the extended polygonal chain after $x$ line segments are added?
questions.questionText: A polygonal chain is a connected series of line segments whose length is defined as the sum of the lengths of the line segments it is made of. A polygonal chain with a length of 33 centimeters (cm) is extended by adding line segments that each have a length of $8 - r$ cm. Which function $f$ represents the total length, in cm, of the extended polygonal chain after $x$ line segments are added?

questions.questionText: If $\frac{9}{6}(x-5)-36=\frac{7}{6}(x-5)+28$, what is the value of $x-5$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2

questions.questionText: $30$ grams of compound A, $45$ grams of compound B, and $x$ grams of compound C. The resulting mixture has a mass of $135$ grams. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Line $k$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $j$ (not shown) is perpendicular to line $k$ and passes through the point $(19, 23)$. What is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept of line $j$?
questions.questionText: Line $k$ is shown in the $xy$-plane. Line $j$ (not shown) is perpendicular to line $k$ and passes through the point $(19, 23)$. What is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept of line $j$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: For a study, a group of chipmunks will be selected from a habitat consisting of 220 chipmunks, and a group of prairie dogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 190 prairie dogs. Some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?
questions.questionText: For a study, a group of chipmunks will be selected from a habitat consisting of 220 chipmunks, and a group of prairie dogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 190 prairie dogs. Some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?

choices.text: The number of chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the number of chipmunks in the control group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the control group.
The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly assigned to the treatment and control groups.
The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly selected from their respective habitats.

The average age of the chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the average age of the chipmunks in the control group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the control group.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Akeyla opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$700.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.6% of the original balance. 6 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?
questions.questionText: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Akeyla opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$700.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.6% of the original balance. 6 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: As the size of a firework's shell increases, the height above the ground when the firework bursts increases. For a firework to burst 1,075 feet above the ground, a firework's shell in the shape of a sphere with a volume of 542.68 cubic inches could be used. To the nearest whole number, what is the volume of this firework's shell in $\text{cubic centimeters}$? (1 inch = 2.54 centimeters)
questions.questionText: As the size of a firework's shell increases, the height above the ground when the firework bursts increases. questions.questionText: Scientists collected fallen acorns that each housed a colony of the ant species P. ohioensis and analyzed each colony's structure. For any of these colonies, if the colony has $x$ worker ants, the equation $y = 0.67x + 2.6$, where $20 \leq x \leq 110$, gives the predicted number of larvae, $y$, in the colony. If one of these colonies has 53 worker ants, which of the following is closest to the predicted number of larvae in the colony?

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 40º and the measure of angle $E$ is 95º. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 40º and the measure of angle $S$ is 95º. Which of the following additional pieces of information is needed to determine whether triangle $DEF$ is similar to triangle $RST$?
questions.questionText: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 40º and the measure of angle $E$ is 95º. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 40º and the measure of angle $S$ is 95º. Which of the following additional pieces of information is needed to determine whether triangle $DEF$ is similar to triangle $RST$?

choices.text: The measure of angle $F$ and the measure of angle $T$

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x - 4)^2 + 5$ gives a toy car's height above the ground $f(x)$, in inches, $x$ seconds after it started moving on an elevated track, where $0 \leq x \leq 10$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: The function $f(x) = \frac{1}{9}(x - 4)^2 + 5$ gives a toy car's height above the ground $f(x)$, in inches, $x$ seconds after it started moving on an elevated track, where $0 \leq x \leq 10$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the vertex of the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?

choices.text: The toy car's minimum height was 5 inches above the ground.
The toy car's minimum height was 4 inches above the ground.
The toy car's height was 5 inches above the ground when it started moving.
The toy car's height was 4 inches above the ground when it started moving.

questions.testName: October 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The function $f(t)=30,000(2)^{\frac{t}{370}}$ gives the number of bacteria in a population $t$ minutes after an initial observation. How much time, in minutes, does it take for the number of bacteria in the population to double?

questions.prompt: A polygonal chain is a connected series of line segments whose length is defined as the sum of the lengths of the line segments it is made of. A polygonal chain with a length of 33 centimeters (cm) is extended by adding line segments that each have a length of $8 - r$ cm. Which function $f$ represents the total length, in cm, of the extended polygonal chain after $x$ line segments are added?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: For a study, a group of chipmunks will be selected from a habitat consisting of 220 chipmunks, and a group of prairie dogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 190 prairie dogs. Some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?
questions.questionText: For a study, a group of chipmunks will be selected from a habitat consisting of 220 chipmunks, and a group of prairie dogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 190 prairie dogs. Some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the chipmunks and prairie dogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?

choices.text: The number of chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the number of chipmunks in the control group is equal to the number of prairie dogs in the control group.
The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly assigned to the treatment and control groups.
The chipmunks and the prairie dogs are randomly selected from their respective habitats.

The average age of the chipmunks in the treatment group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the treatment group, and the average age of the chipmunks in the control group is equal to the average age of the prairie dogs in the control group.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Evaluating statistical claims: Observational studies and experiments

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Akeyla opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$700.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.6% of the original balance. 6 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?
questions.questionText: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Akeyla opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$700.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.6% of the original balance. 6 months after Akeyla opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.1% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: As the size of a firework's shell increases, the height above the ground when the firework bursts increases. For a firework to burst 1,075 feet above the ground, a firework's shell in the shape of a sphere with a volume of 542.68 cubic inches could be used. To the nearest whole number, what is the volume of this firework's shell in $\text{cubic centimeters}$? (1 inch = 2.54 centimeters)
